items.title: Introduction
items.description: Health Assistant — a self-hosted, open-source, privacy-first health records platform. Centralize labs, visits, medications, and wearable data with an AI assistant that proposes but never writes.

items.title: Visual Tour
items.description: Screenshots of Health Assistant, the self-hosted health records platform — dashboard, biomarker trends, AI chat, examinations, and patient views.

items.title: Self-Hosted Installation
items.description: Install Health Assistant, a self-hosted, open-source health records platform. Docker quick start, production deployment, environment setup with auto-generated keys, and a security checklist.

items.title: Architecture Overview
items.description: Architecture of Health Assistant, a self-hosted, open-source health records platform: FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB data model, HL7 FHIR R4 storage, biomarker engine, AI/OCR pipeline, and React frontend.

items.title: Repository Structure
items.description: Codebase layout of Health Assistant, the self-hosted, open-source health records platform: FastAPI backend, React/Vite frontend, Integrations SDK, and documentation.

items.title: Users, Tenants & Roles
items.description: Multi-tenant user management in Health Assistant, a self-hosted, open-source health records platform. Tenant isolation, organizational hierarchies, role-based access control, and identity-to-clinical-record linking.

items.title: Ontology & Catalog
items.description: The JSON-based clinical ontology catalog format used by Health Assistant, a self-hosted health records platform. Bulk-import LOINC biomarkers, medical definitions, and unit conversions.

items.title: Taxonomy & Knowledge Graph
items.description: The unified medical taxonomy and knowledge graph in Health Assistant, a self-hosted health records platform. Multi-kind concepts, polymorphic edges, and cross-domain graph traversal.

items.title: Clinical Events & Journeys
items.description: Longitudinal clinical events in Health Assistant, a self-hosted, open-source health records platform. Seed JSON schema, schedule_kind rendering, metadata_schema field types, categories, and localization for journeys like pregnancies, chronic pain, and surgical recovery.

items.title: Notification System
items.description: The unified notification system of Health Assistant, a self-hosted health records platform. Multi-source fan-out, biomarker alert rules, Web Push (VAPID), scheduled reminders, digest collapsing, and real-time WebSocket delivery.

items.title: Telemetry & TimescaleDB
items.description: High-frequency health data handling in Health Assistant, a self-hosted health records platform. The TimescaleDB telemetry split, dynamic biomarker routing, and OHLC dashboard aggregation.

items.title: Export & Import (Backup)
items.description: Backup and restore for Health Assistant, a self-hosted health records platform. FHIR R4B Bundle + BagIt-style ZIP exports, SHA256 integrity checks, and cross-tenant id remapping.

items.title: AI System & Configuration
items.description: The AI architecture of Health Assistant, a self-hosted health records platform. Provider-agnostic factory, OCR/NLP pipeline, agentic chatbot with human-in-the-loop proposals, and per-tenant model configuration.

items.title: REST API Reference
items.description: REST API reference for Health Assistant, the self-hosted, open-source health records platform. Authentication, patients, observations, biomarkers, medications, clinical events, and FHIR endpoints.

items.title: FHIR R4 Facade
items.description: The FHIR R4 REST API facade of Health Assistant, a self-hosted health records platform. 19 conformance resources, CapabilityStatement, search Bundles, Provenance-on-write, and OperationOutcome errors.

items.title: Integrations Framework
items.description: The pluggable Integrations Framework of Health Assistant, a self-hosted health records platform. Connect wearables, labs, webhooks, and FHIR servers; opt-in hooks for clinical events, examinations, catalog proposals, HITL review, document pull, rate-limit backoff, and notification digests.

items.title: Integrations SDK
items.description: Developer guide for the Health Assistant Integrations SDK — build custom health data providers for the self-hosted, open-source platform with connection pooling, rate-limit cooldown, cursor sync, OAuth2+PKCE, webhook HMAC helpers, clinical events, examinations, catalog proposals, HITL review, document pull with DB dedup, and notification digests.

items.title: Mobile Sync App
items.description: Architecture for a headless mobile companion app that syncs on-device health data (Android Health Connect, iOS HealthKit) to a self-hosted Health Assistant instance.

items.title: Development Guide
items.description: Set up a local development environment for Health Assistant, the self-hosted, open-source health records platform. Covers the FastAPI backend, React frontend, Celery worker, testing, migrations, and code style.

items.title: CI/CD Deployment
items.description: CI/CD pipeline setup for Health Assistant, a self-hosted, open-source health records platform. Gitea Actions workflow, required secrets, and homelab deployment caveats.

items.title: Seeding & Demo Data
items.description: How to create seed and demo data for Health Assistant, a self-hosted health records platform. Idempotent seeding patterns, FHIR Bundle imports, and deterministic dates for screenshots.

items.title: Task Debugging Guide
items.description: Debugging guide for stuck documents and OCR extraction tasks in Health Assistant, a self-hosted health records platform. Diagnose failed pipeline stages and recover data.

items.title: Status & Beta Roadmap
items.description: Current implementation status of Health Assistant, the self-hosted, open-source health records platform. What's done, what's in progress, and what's planned.

items.title: Development Roadmap
items.description: Roadmap for Health Assistant, the self-hosted, open-source health records platform: biomarker clinical insights, mobile companion app, advanced FHIR R4 conformance, and end-to-end testing.
